description: Draws a curved or straight arrow between two points on the canvas, with optional curvature, MathJax text label at the arrow end, and text offset control via buff().
utility: Draw animated curved arrows between any two points with optional text labels

arguments.name: from
arguments.description: Source endpoint — direct coordinates, shape anchor (0-8), or shape variable (center)

arguments.name: to
arguments.type: at(row, col) | at(shape, anchor) | variable | dx,dy
arguments.description: Destination endpoint — same types as from, or two numbers for delta offset mode

arguments.name: curvature
arguments.description: Arc curvature in logical units. Positive = clockwise, negative = counter-clockwise, 0 = straight

arguments.name: text
arguments.description: MathJax text label rendered at the arrow end

arguments.name: buff
arguments.description: Text offset control. First arg: offset along arrow direction (positive = further from tip). Second arg: offset perpendicular (positive = right side of arrow)

alternates.description: Arrow from center of shape A to center of shape B
Curved arrow with curvature of 2 logical units
Arrow with MathJax text at the arrow end
Text offset 0.3 units along arrow direction
Text offset: 2 back along arrow, 1 right of arrow
Arrow from (5,3) offset by dx=3, dy=2
Arrow from G's right edge (4) to M's left edge (8)

examples.scenario: Arrow annotating a plottable curve on a graph (plot-table-version2 lesson)
Arrow with text label for major axis length
examples.expression: arrow_1 = arrow(at(12,44), at(9,40), "text(major axis length is 10)", buff(10, -3.5), s(3), c(darkblue), defer(), t(1))

examples.scenario: Arrow annotating minor axis on ellipse
examples.expression: arrow_3 = arrow(at(19,28), at(22,23), "text(minor axis)", s(3), buff(5.5, 3.5))

examples.scenario: Arrow for rearranging equation with stroke width
examples.expression: arrow_2 = arrow(at(19,36), at(21,41), s(2), notes("Rearrange the Equation", 0.5))

examples.scenario: Arrow from annotation to fadeout with power rule
